item.title: The best AirPods deals you can get right now
item.description:
Source: The Verge
If you know where to look, you can often score deals on Apple’s ever-expanding AirPods lineup. Both the AirPods Max and AirPods 4 (with and without ANC) now consistently receive discounts, as do the newer AirPods Pro 3. And while more recent shopping events like Black Friday and Cyber Monday delivered some of the steepest […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: X says it will suspend creators from revenue-sharing program for unlabeled AI posts of ‘armed conflict’
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Creators who break the rules will get a three-month suspension, and if they continue to violate the policy, they'll be permanently banned.
Matched: policy(+3), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Hacked traffic cams and hijacked TVs: How cyber operations supported the war against Iran
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
After U.S. and Israeli forces started bombing Iran, reports say cyber operations have disrupted communications, supported surveillance activities, and have been used in psychological operations.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Highguard is shutting down this month
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Another high-profile live-service game is shutting down soon after launch: this time it's the free-to-play squad shooter Highguard. Developer Wildlight Entertainment says that, even though Highguard reached 2 million players, the game will be shut down permanently on March 12th. That's less than two months after the game debuted on January 26th. "Despite the passion […]
Matched: ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: Anduril aims at $60 billion valuation in new funding round
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The funding round would come less than a year after Anduril's Series G, which closed in June with $2.5 billion against a $30 billion valuation.
Matched: ai(+3), funding(+2)

item.title: Apple’s rumored MacBook Neo, a lower-cost, colorful laptop, could launch this week
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
This would mark the first time Apple competes with cheaper hardware like the Chromebook.
Matched: war(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: Another Oracle outage is messing up US TikTok
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The US version of TikTok is once again experiencing issues due to an Oracle outage, just a month after coming back online from a similar outage in February. The service disruption, impacting Oracle's Ashburn, Virginia data center, began early Tuesday afternoon, with Downdetector reports spiking around 1PM Eastern. TikTok USDS confirmed in a post on […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Shark’s latest robot vacuum hunts stains with UV light
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The Shark PowerDetect UV Reveal is SharkNinja's latest robot vacuum and mop. A flagship model with a multifunctional dock that empties the dustbin and refills and washes its mop, the Reveal's signature feature is a UV light designed to "find" stains on your floors. It costs $1,299.99 and is available now. Combined with an RGB […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Claude Code rolls out a voice mode capability
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Anthropic is stepping up its game in the AI coding space with the rollout of Voice Mode in Claude Code.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Android users can now share tracker tag info with airlines to help locate lost luggage
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
More than 10 global airlines now accept Find Hub locations as part of their baggage recovery process.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Android’s Find Hub adds iPhone-like luggage tracking links
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Google is rolling out an Android update that includes the ability to share the location of your missing luggage with an airline. It's similar to the luggage-tracking feature Apple brought to the iPhone, allowing you to view the location of your Find Hub tag or accessory on a map, and provide your airline with a […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Google’s latest Pixel drop allows Gemini to order groceries for you and more
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Google is adding several new features to Pixel phones with its latest March update, including the ability for its Gemini AI assistant to do things for you, like order groceries or book a ride. This feature, which was first shown off during Samsung's Unpacked event last week, is rolling out now to the Pixel 10, […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: The Pixel Watch now lets you tap to pay without opening the Wallet app
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Google has a big update in store for the Pixel Watch, which will now allow you to make a range of payments by simply tapping your watch to a retail terminal without opening the Wallet app. The feature, called "express pay," is coming to the Pixel Watch 2 and later. It's similar to "express mode" […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Phone makers of all sizes are feeling the RAM crunch
item.description:
Source: The Verge
We've been talking to phone companies both big and small this week at MWC, and they've basically all agreed on one point: the RAM crisis is hitting hard, and phone prices will almost certainly increase where they haven't already. For a major global brand like Xiaomi, volume is one lever the company can pull. To […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: The new MacBook Pro laptops are as much as $400 more expensive than their predecessors. Thank the RAM shortage.
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
With the demands for more computers and data centers to power AI, the market is experiencing a shortage of RAM, causing memory prices to surge.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Activist investor Elliott takes a $1B stake in Pinterest, betting on AI-driven growth
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The news comes as Pinterest has faced serious headwinds. Shares tumbled over the past year, with disappointing earnings, layoffs affecting 15% of its workforce, a declining ad business, and increased rivalry from AI chatbots.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Apple unveils new MacBook Air and MacBook Pro with M5
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
These updated M5 chips were specifically designed to make the MacBook Air and MacBook Pro laptops better at handling intensive AI tasks.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Yahoo is selling Engadget to Static Media
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Engadget, the long-running tech news website, has a new owner. Yahoo sold the publication to Static Media in a deal that was signed in early February and is scheduled to close later this March. (Terms of the deal weren't disclosed.) The deal hasn't yet been publicly announced, but a source familiar with the publication says […]
Matched: who(+2)

item.title: Audible launches a cheaper ‘Standard’ subscription plan, challenging Spotify
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Under the new Standard plan, subscribers will lose access to the audiobooks they've consumed when they unsubscribe. The Premium plan lets users keep the audiobooks they’ve listened to even if they unsubscribe.
Matched: launch(+2)

item.title: India’s Pronto formalizes house help as its valuation jumps 8x in under a year
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Pronto handles 18,000 daily bookings and plans rapid expansion as it races rivals to digitize India's largely offline home services market.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Cursor has reportedly surpassed $2B in annualized revenue
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The four-year-old startup saw its revenue run rate double over the past three months, according to one Bloomberg source.
Matched: startup(+2)

item.title: ChatGPT’s new GPT-5.3 Instant model will stop telling you to calm down
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The company says the new model will reduce the "cringe" that's been annoying its users for months.

item.title: Google brings Android’s desktop mode to Pixel devices
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Google is bringing a new desktop mode to its Pixel phones and tablets. The feature comes as part of Google's March Pixel drop, allowing users with a Pixel 8 and newer to connect their device to an external monitor via USB-C for a "desktop-like multi-window experience." You can use a mouse and keyboard with the […]

item.title: Amid new competition, Chrome speeds up its release schedule
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Google Chrome will begin shipping new releases every two weeks in September, instead of monthly.
